area_anchor: Brikette — Loading earlier messages can skip or repeat messages when new emails arrive during browsing
current_truth: Cursor-based pagination uses message IDs as cursors, but IDs may not be strictly monotonic with timestamps. If new messages arrive while staff are loading earlier messages, the cursor position can shift, causing messages to be skipped or repeated.
next_scope_now: Use a composite cursor (timestamp + ID) for stable pagination ordering, ensuring no messages are skipped or repeated regardless of concurrent inserts.
evidence_refs: operator-stated: cursor pagination race when IDs not monotonic with timestamps
why: When staff scroll through a long email thread and click 'load earlier messages', new incoming emails can shift the cursor position. This means some messages might be skipped or shown twice, which is confusing when trying to read the full conversation history.

intended_outcome.statement: Message pagination uses a stable composite cursor that prevents skipped or repeated messages during concurrent email arrival.
